message:
Skip policy check for automated submodule bumps

The policy enforcement check for submodule bumps can fail due to a race
condition: when a PR is merged to tenzir-plugins and the auto-bumper
immediately creates a submodule bump commit, the CI's git fetch may get
a stale origin/main before the merge fully propagates.

Skip the check entirely for commits authored by tenzir-bot with the
subject "Bump submodule from...". These automated bumps only occur after
a PR has been merged to tenzir-plugins, so the review has already
happened. The policy exists to catch humans manually bumping submodules
without a corresponding PR.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -276,6 +276,14 @@ jobs:
         env:
           GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.generate_token.outputs.token }}
         run: |
+          # Skip check for automated submodule bumps
+          commit_author=$(git log -1 --format='%an')
+          commit_subject=$(git log -1 --format='%s')
+          if [[ "$commit_author" == "tenzir-bot" && "$commit_subject" == "Bump submodule from"* ]]; then
+            echo "Skipping policy check for automated bump"
+            exit 0
+          fi
+
           plugins_sha=$(git -C contrib/tenzir-plugins rev-parse HEAD)
           plugins_main_sha=$(git -C contrib/tenzir-plugins rev-parse origin/main)
